sub_task: Verify Python JSON usage patterns (no echo/printf JSON)

python3_json_patterns.summary: Project follows Python-for-JSON convention across 4 shell scripts
issues: Unsafe python3 -c shell interpolation with single quotes can break on special chars
Repeated Python process spawning is inefficient
python3_json_patterns.recommendation: Use sys.argv/heredoc for paths, batch operations, temp+replace writes

echo_printf_antipattern.detail: Single instance in skills/SKILL.md is documentation example, not runtime code
echo_printf_antipattern.recommendation: Update doc example to use python3 -c pattern for consistency

json_dump_usage.summary: Mixed atomic vs non-atomic writes, missing explicit UTF-8
risky_patterns: Direct overwrites with w mode: stampede.sh:353,830,842
json_dump_usage.recommendation: Add explicit UTF-8 encoding, convert direct writes to atomic pattern

json_load_usage.summary: Critical crash risks in merge script - 5 unprotected json.load calls
crash_prone: stampede-merge.sh:318 - result files in loop - one bad file kills merge
json_load_usage.recommendation: Wrap all json.load in try/except with sensible defaults, add per-file handling in loops

json_error_handling.summary: JSONDecodeError only caught specifically in docs, not in production code
unhandled_crash_risks: smoke-test.sh:50,117,135 - will crash on malformed JSON
json_error_handling.recommendation: Catch JSONDecodeError specifically, avoid broad except swallowing

overall_assessment.convention_compliance: HIGH - Project correctly uses Python for JSON, no echo/printf violations in production
overall_assessment.error_handling: MEDIUM - Many locations have try/except but too broad; merge script is most vulnerable
overall_assessment.atomicity: MEDIUM - Some good temp+replace patterns, some risky direct overwrites
top_priority_fixes: Add try/except to 5 unprotected json.load calls in stampede-merge.sh
Switch from except Exception to except JSONDecodeError where appropriate
